message:
feat: Add production error handling and Google Analytics tracking

Implements comprehensive error boundaries and analytics infrastructure
to improve production stability and user insights.

Changes:
- Add ErrorBoundary component with graceful error UI and logging
- Add GoogleAnalytics component using Next.js Script optimization
- Add ThreeSceneLoading skeleton component for better UX
- Add .env.example with GA setup documentation

Technical details:
- ErrorBoundary uses class component pattern for getDerivedStateFromError
- GA only loads in production to avoid development noise
- Loading skeleton matches Three.js scene dimensions (280px-460px)
- Environment variables documented with clear examples

Impact:
- Prevents white screen crashes from component errors
- Provides user-friendly fallback UI when errors occur
- Enables tracking of site usage and user behavior
- Improves perceived loading performance with skeletons

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+# Google Analytics
+# ================================================================================
+# Your Google Analytics 4 Measurement ID (format: G-XXXXXXXXXX)
+#
+# HOW TO GET THIS:
+# 1. Go to https://analytics.google.com/
+# 2. Create a new GA4 property or use an existing one
+# 3. Go to Admin > Data Streams > Web
+# 4. Click your web stream
+# 5. Copy the "Measurement ID" (starts with G-)
+#
+# NOTE: This is NOT a secret! GA tracking IDs are meant to be public and visible
+# in client-side code. We use an env var for flexibility, not security.
+#
+# DOMAIN SETUP:
+# - You can set this up NOW even before your domain transfer completes
+# - GA will track whatever domain your site runs on (localhost, vercel.app, jeffreyemanuel.com)
+# - Once jeffreyemanuel.com points to your site, GA will automatically show that domain
+# - No need to wait for DNS to propagate
+#
+# DEPLOYMENT:
+# - On Vercel: Add this in Project Settings > Environment Variables
+# - Locally: Copy this file to .env.local and fill in your GA ID
+#
+NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_ID=
+
+# Example (replace with your actual ID):
+# NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_ID=G-ABC123XYZ
